item.title: Enable Private DNS on Android TV
item.description: Android comes with a setting called Private DNS that allows us to use DNS-over-TLS instead of a regular DNS as the system-wide domain resolver. However, because that setting is hidden on Android TV, enabling it there is not as easy as enabling it on Android smartphones. But thanks to Android’s Developer Mode, we can enable it on Android TV via ADB command. The following below are the steps to enable it there.

item.title: About
item.description: About Hello there! 👋 Welcome to my site, where I put my experiments and random thoughts to revisit them later in case I forget. I hope you can find something useful here too. My latest post is titled Enable Private DNS on Android TV. Bio My name is Rudy Susanto. I live in Sidoarjo, Indonesia. I’m a web developer at PeepSo, working on WordPress plugins and theme development. Contact You can reach me at rudy (𝑎𝑡) rsusanto (𝑑𝑜𝑡) com.

item.title: Hugo: Quick and Easy Setup
item.description: We will start this tutorial with a TL;DR section to get a simple Hugo project up and running, followed by the remaining sections to describe it. TL;DR These are a few CLI commands which I summarize from the official Hugo documentation page to get a simple Hugo project up and running. # Install Hugo static site generator brew install hugo # Create a new project hugo new site homepage cd homepage git init # Download and select preferred theme git submodule add https://github.
